paragraphs.text: We welcome the possibility of consultation and the possibility to contribute to the preparatory work of the Digital Fairness Act. In the face of increasing digitalisation and the challenges it poses to the European population, we stress the need for a Digital Fairness Act that focuses in particular on the fundamental rights of vulnerable groups. In this context, the City of Vienna advocates strict regulation of exploitative and addictive design and profiling, but above all fundamental rights such as the right to privacy and non-discrimination in the digital sphere. The Digital Fairness Act provides an opportunity to go beyond existing laws and prioritise the protection of the rights of minors, a highly vulnerable group. Working together to regulate the minimum age for social media to protect children from harm in the digital space, in particular in the context of consumer protection. It is essential that age limits are actually enforceable and sanctionable. Clear and enforceable provisions can protect children and young people from manipulative marketing tactics. In particular, companies must be obliged to take care of children and young people. The European Union must act decisively to protect vulnerable groups, especially minors, in the digital space. A comprehensive Digital Fairness Act is a crucial step in this direction.
